backlog.title: Multi-turn generation fallback (v2.3, G21)
backlog.description: Implement the lossless multi-turn generation fallback (PRD §9.24, FR-T1–FR-T12) for the MESSAGE role on the single-commit path (§13.1–§13.5). When a one-shot generation of a large diff fails (provider per-request unreliability below the advertised context window), losslessly re-deliver the FULL captured diff across request-sized provider session turns so a single commit message can still be produced — without truncation and without decomposing into multiple commits. This is strictly a fallback inserted between one-shot-retry-exhaustion and the existing rescue protocol; it reuses parse/dedupe/rescue/CAS/lock UNCHANGED and changes NO CLI flags, exit codes, or rescue messages. Scope is message-role only (planner/stager/arbiter and the decompose path are out of scope per FR-T10). FR-T9 (the pi `session_mode = "append"` append-turn rendering) is already verified empirically — see plan/009_5c53066d64b3/architecture/fr-t9-verification.md.

milestones.title: Lossless multi-turn fallback for the message role
milestones.description: Deliver the four implementing requirements (R1 provider surface, R2 config surface, R3 generate core, R4 tests) plus a final Mode B documentation sync. Each requirement composes onto the existing one-shot pipeline without forking parse/dedupe/rescue. On completion, a pi-backed run whose one-shot generation exhausts on a large diff will transparently fall back to N+1 session turns, produce a single commit message from the full diff, and on any failure degrade to the identical rescue a one-shot failure would produce.

tasks.title: Provider surface: session_mode manifest field + pi value + multi-turn render variant (R1)
tasks.description: Add the `session_mode` manifest field (FR-T8), set pi's shipped value to "append" (FR-T6/T9 — already verified), and add a multi-turn render variant that drops pi's `--no-session`, adds `--session-id <id>`, and emits the system-prompt flag on turn 1 only. This task is the foundation: R3's trigger gate condition (d) and the multi-turn protocol both depend on the new field and render path. Reuses Manifest's pointer-scalar convention, MergeManifest's regime-1 scalar merge, and Render's §12.2 token pipeline; adds a SIBLING RenderMultiTurn method (not a widened Render signature) to keep all 24+ existing Render call sites byte-identical.

subtasks.title: Add SessionMode field to Manifest struct + Resolve() default + Validate() enum
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: plan/009_5c53066d64b3/architecture/research-provider.md §1 — the Manifest struct (internal/provider/manifest.go) uses a pointer-scalar convention: go-toml/v2 has no omitempty, so optional SCALAR fields are *string/*bool so an ABSENT user override decodes to nil (inherit the built-in on merge) while a PRESENT value (even ""/false) overrides. SessionMode MUST follow this convention. PRD §12.1 fixes the TOML ordering: session_mode sits between provider_flag and bare_flags (PRD.md:726–733).
2. INPUT: PRD §9.24 FR-T8 (session_mode field, "" default | "append"), FR-T9 (verification duty); §12.1 manifest schema.
3. LOGIC: Add to internal/provider/manifest.go — a new `// --- session continuation (multi-turn fallback, §9.24) ---` block between the `// --- sub-provider ---` block (ProviderFlag, line ~60–61) and `// --- bare mode ---` (BareFlags, line ~63–64). Field: `SessionMode *string \`toml:"session_mode"\`` with a doc comment matching the PRD §12.1 block ("" default = no support; "append" = re-invoking the same session id appends a recallable turn; REQUIRES verified rendering FR-T9; never speculatively). In Resolve() (~line 110–164), add `if out.SessionMode == nil { out.SessionMode = strPtr("") }` mirroring the ProviderFlag/PrintFlag clauses. In Validate() (~line 88–106), add: `if m.SessionMode != nil && *m.SessionMode != "" && *m.SessionMode != "append" { return fmt.Errorf("provider manifest %q: session_mode %q must be \"\" or \"append\"", m.Name, *m.SessionMode) }` (enum enforcement; nil passes — the absent case).
4. OUTPUT: Manifest.SessionMode field exists; Resolve() guarantees non-nil after resolution (safe `*r.SessionMode` deref for render/trigger); Validate() rejects bogus values. Consumed by P1.M1.T1.S3 (RenderMultiTurn capability gate), P1.M1.T1.S2 (MergeManifest), and P1.M1.T3.S3 (trigger gate condition d).
5. DOCS: none — the manifest-schema doc update rides with P1.M1.T1.S5 (Mode A). This subtask is the struct/behavior change only.

subtasks.title: Add MergeManifest scalar clause for SessionMode (config-overridable field merge)
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: research-provider.md §4 — MergeManifest lives in internal/provider/merge.go (NOT registry.go) at line 28; NewRegistry (registry.go:42–55) calls it per override key. Regime-1 (scalar pointer fields, merge.go:54–95) does `if override.X != nil { out.X = override.X }`. A plain *string SessionMode merges IDENTICALLY to every other scalar — confirmed.
2. INPUT: P1.M1.T1.S1 (the SessionMode *string field). PRD §16.1 / FR-37a — [provider.<name>] blocks field-merge across layers.
3. LOGIC: In internal/provider/merge.go regime-1 (the scalar block, ~line 80 right after the ProviderFlag clause), add: `if override.SessionMode != nil { out.SessionMode = override.SessionMode }`. This gives FR-37a semantics: a user who sets `session_mode = ""` in `[provider.pi]` overrides the built-in "append" (disables multi-turn for pi); a user who omits the key inherits the built-in. A user setting `session_mode = "append"` on a provider whose built-in ships "" (claude etc.) overrides up — their explicit choice; the FR-T9 duty is on the SHIPPED default, not user config.
4. OUTPUT: SessionMode threads through the config-override merge (global→repo→git-config [provider.*] blocks). Consumed transitively by Registry.Get → Manifest.Render/RenderMultiTurn.
5. DOCS: none — config provider-section doc rides with P1.M1.T1.S5.

subtasks.title: Add RenderMultiTurn sibling method (drop --no-session, add --session-id, sys prompt turn-1-only)
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: research-provider.md §2 — Render is `func (m Manifest) Render(model, sysPrompt, userPayload, reasoning string, mode ...RenderMode) (*CmdSpec, error)` at render.go:89; it is variadic-mode (default RenderBare) so 24+ call sites stay byte-identical. There is NO existing mechanism to drop a specific bare_flag token (BareFlags is opaque: `args = append(args, r.BareFlags...)`). The §12.2 token order is: subcommand → provider_flag split → model_flag → reasoning → system_prompt_flag → (bare|tooled flags) → print_flag → payload-by-delivery. fr-t9-verification.md records the VERIFIED pi flag set (2026-07-05): pi BareFlags MINUS --no-session, PLUS --session-id <id>; system prompt via --system-prompt on turn 1 only; -p kept; --continue/-c NOT used.
2. INPUT: P1.M1.T1.S1 (Manifest.SessionMode). PRD §9.24 FR-T6 (turn protocol rendering), FR-T8/T9 (capability gate). PRD §12.2 token order.
3. LOGIC: Add a SIBLING method in internal/provider/render.go (do NOT widen Render's signature — blast radius 24+ sites). Signature: `func (m Manifest) RenderMultiTurn(model, sysPrompt, userPayload, reasoning, sessionID string, turn int) (*CmdSpec, error)`. It calls m.Validate() + m.Resolve() like Render, then rebuilds the §12.2 args EXACTLY as Render does (subcommand → provider_flag/model slash-split → model_flag → reasoning tokens → system_prompt_flag WHEN turn==1 && sysPrompt!="" (turn-1-only — FR-T6: the session carries it) → bare-flags block with the multi-turn substitution → print_flag → payload-by-delivery). The substitution: build a filtered copy of r.BareFlags excluding the exact token "--no-session", then append "--session-id", sessionID after that block. Capability gate: `if *r.SessionMode != "append" { return nil, fmt.Errorf("provider %q: multi-turn render requires session_mode=\"append\"", m.Name) }`. The unified-payload + delivery switch is identical to Render (stdin→spec.Stdin; positional→trailing arg; flag→prompt_flag). When turn > 1 the sysPrompt arg is "" so no system_prompt_flag is emitted AND the payload is NOT prepended with sys (the session already has it).
4. OUTPUT: RenderMultiTurn(*CmdSpec, error) for one multi-turn turn; reused by P1.M1.T3.S2 (the protocol calls it per turn). Keeps Render and every existing caller byte-identical.
5. MOCKING: none — pure data transformation (no spawning; the os.Environ() Env side effect matches Render).
6. DOCS: none — render-behavior doc rides with P1.M1.T1.S5.

subtasks.title: Set SessionMode="append" on pi builtin + providers/pi.toml (FR-T9 verified)
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: fr-t9-verification.md — FR-T9 is ALREADY VERIFIED empirically (2026-07-05): `pi --provider zai --model glm-5.2 --no-tools --no-extensions --no-skills --no-prompt-templates --no-context-files --session-id stagehand-frt9-probe -p "remember the word BANANA"` then a second call with the SAME --session-id asking to recall returns "BANANA". The append-turn rendering holds. research-provider.md §3 — builtinPi() is at internal/provider/builtin.go:30–96; BareFlags (line ~60) contains "--no-session" (the flag RenderMultiTurn drops); the `# VERIFIED <date> via \`<cmd>\`` inline-comment discipline (FR-D5) is the established pattern (e.g. builtin.go:47 ListModelsCommand).
2. INPUT: P1.M1.T1.S1 (SessionMode field). fr-t9-verification.md (the verified flag set + date).
3. LOGIC: In internal/provider/builtin.go builtinPi(), add `SessionMode: strPtr("append"),` with an inline trailing comment recording the verification: `// VERIFIED 2026-07-05 via \`pi --session-id X <isolation-flags-minus-no-session> -p "remember BANANA"\` then recall returns BANANA; FR-T9.` In providers/pi.toml add `session_mode = "append"` (in the same TOML-ordering position as the manifest struct, between provider_flag and bare_flags per PRD §12.1) with the matching `# VERIFIED 2026-07-05; FR-T9.` comment. Do NOT set SessionMode on any other builtin (claude/opencode/codex/cursor/agy/gemini/qwen-code) — they ship absent and Resolve() defaults to "".
4. OUTPUT: pi manifest declares session_mode="append"; every other provider ships "". This makes FR-T1 condition (d) true ONLY for pi, so multi-turn activates only for pi until another provider is verified.
5. DOCS: none — the providers.md session_mode doc rides with P1.M1.T1.S5.

subtasks.title: Render unit tests (golden/table) + Mode A provider docs (providers.md, configuration.md)
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: research-tests-ui.md §5 — internal/provider/render_test.go is golden/table-driven; TestRender_GoldenPerProvider (lines 36–90) and TestRender_Pi_ByteForByteCommitPi (94–108) are the templates; helpers containsPair/containsToken (396–416) assert a flag-value pair or single token; TestRender_DoesNotMutateManifest (311–322) is the non-mutation regression guard. The pattern: a cases slice of {name, m, model, wantCmd, wantArgs, wantStdin} under t.Run, asserting spec.Command, reflect.DeepEqual(spec.Args, wantArgs), spec.Stdin.
2. INPUT: P1.M1.T1.S3 (RenderMultiTurn), P1.M1.T1.S4 (pi SessionMode="append"). PRD §9.24 FR-T6/T8.
3. LOGIC: Add table-driven unit tests in internal/provider/render_test.go (or a new render_multiturn_test.go) for RenderMultiTurn: (a) pi turn 1 — assert args contain "--session-id", <stableID>, do NOT contain "--no-session", contain "--system-prompt", <sys>, contain "-p"; (b) pi turn 2 — same minus the system-prompt flag (turn-1-only); (c) the sessionID passed in equals the one in args (stable across turns); (d) a non-append provider (claude, SessionMode="") → RenderMultiTurn returns an error; (e) TestRender_DoesNotMutateManifest-style guard that RenderMultiTurn does not mutate the caller's Manifest (BareFlags slice untouched — the --no-session filter must build a NEW slice, not edit in place). Do NOT assert exact arg position for --session-id (the stub test in P1.M1.T4 asserts presence/absence, not order); assert presence + the system-prompt turn-1-only distinction.
4. OUTPUT: passing unit tests proving the render variant's contract; a green `go test ./internal/provider/...`.
5. DOCS: Mode A (rides with this work) — (i) docs/providers.md: document the `session_mode` manifest field under the manifest-schema section, that ONLY pi declares "append" today (verified 2026-07-05 per FR-T9), and that adding another provider requires the FR-T9 empirical append-turn verification bar (cross-link §9.24). (ii) docs/configuration.md `[provider.<name>]` section: add a `session_mode` row if provider-level override is surfaced there, mirroring how other manifest fields are listed (state the override semantics from P1.M1.T1.S2).

tasks.title: Config surface: multi_turn_fallback + multi_turn_chunk_tokens (R2)
tasks.description: Add the two config knobs that gate and size the multi-turn fallback: `multi_turn_fallback` (bool, default true — FR-T1c) and `multi_turn_chunk_tokens` (int, default 32000 — FR-T3). These are config-file-only (NO CLI flags, NO env vars per the delta). Thread them through the resolved Config, Defaults(), the file-config struct, materialize, and overlay following the EXACT established patterns (int `!= 0` guard for chunk tokens; bool only-true-propagates for fallback — the accepted v1 limitation shared with AutoStageAll/Push).

subtasks.title: Add MultiTurnFallback + MultiTurnChunkTokens to resolved Config struct + Defaults()
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: research-generate-config.md §2 — type Config is at internal/config/config.go:63 (flat, resolved, plain-typed per its docstring lines 47–61). The generation scalars live at lines 78–96; TokenLimit is :81, MaxDuplicateRetries is :83. Defaults() is at config.go:161; the relevant block sets TokenLimit:0, DiffContext:intPtr(1), MaxDuplicateRetries:3, SubjectTargetChars:50 (lines ~174–177). TOML tags are snake_case leaf names kept for documentation parity (the resolved Config is never directly decoded — fileConfig is).
2. INPUT: PRD §9.24 FR-T1c (multi_turn_fallback default true), FR-T3 (multi_turn_chunk_tokens default 32000). §16.1/§16.2 config model.
3. LOGIC: In internal/config/config.go, add two fields to the Config struct immediately after MaxDuplicateRetries (line ~83): `MultiTurnFallback bool \`toml:"multi_turn_fallback"\`` with a comment citing §9.24 FR-T1c, and `MultiTurnChunkTokens int \`toml:"multi_turn_chunk_tokens"\`` citing §9.24 FR-T3. In Defaults() (line ~161), add after the MaxDuplicateRetries entry: `MultiTurnFallback: true,` and `MultiTurnChunkTokens: 32000,` with inline comments naming the FR refs and defaults.
4. OUTPUT: Config.MultiTurnFallback (default true) and Config.MultiTurnChunkTokens (default 32000) exist and are set by Defaults(). Consumed by P1.M1.T2.S2 (file/overlay), P1.M1.T3.S2 (protocol reads cfg.MultiTurnChunkTokens), P1.M1.T3.S3 (trigger gate reads cfg.MultiTurnFallback).
5. DOCS: none — the [generation] table doc rides with P1.M1.T2.S3.

subtasks.title: Add fields to fileGeneration struct + materialize + overlay
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: research-generate-config.md §3 — fileGeneration is at internal/config/file.go:44 (the FILE decode twin; only file.go decodes into it). TokenLimit :52, MaxDuplicateRetries :54. materialize (the delta's "loadTOML overlay") is at file.go:193 — int-guard template `if g.TokenLimit != 0 { c.TokenLimit = g.TokenLimit }` (line ~219), bool-guard template `if d.AutoStageAll { c.AutoStageAll = true }` (line ~213, the v1 limitation: only true propagates). overlay (the delta's "merge") is at file.go:294 — int `if src.TokenLimit != 0 { dst.TokenLimit = src.TokenLimit }` (~332), bool `if src.AutoStageAll { dst.AutoStageAll = true }` (~318).
2. INPUT: P1.M1.T2.S1 (Config fields + Defaults). PRD §9.24 FR-T1c/T3, §16.1 resolution order.
3. LOGIC: (a) In fileGeneration (file.go:44) add `MultiTurnFallback bool \`toml:"multi_turn_fallback"\`` and `MultiTurnChunkTokens int \`toml:"multi_turn_chunk_tokens"\`` after MaxDuplicateRetries (line ~54). (b) In materialize (file.go:193) add two clauses mirroring the templates: `if g.MultiTurnChunkTokens != 0 { c.MultiTurnChunkTokens = g.MultiTurnChunkTokens }` (int template — for a positive default 32000 and positive user values, != 0 and > 0 are equivalent; use != 0 to match house style) and `if g.MultiTurnFallback { c.MultiTurnFallback = true }` (bool template — only-true-propagates; ACCEPTED limitation per the delta: a user setting multi_turn_fallback=false in a file is silently ignored, same as AutoStageAll). (c) In overlay (file.go:294) add the same two guards after the MaxDuplicateRetries block (~345). NOTE: there is NO merge() function in internal/config — the delta's "merge" IS overlay (file.go:294); materialize IS the "loadTOML overlay".
4. OUTPUT: fileConfig decodes the two keys; materialize copies file→resolved; overlay threads them through global→repo→git-config precedence. Env/flag layers are OUT OF SCOPE (no new CLI flags/env per the delta).
5. DOCS: none — the [generation] table + FR-T12 note ride with P1.M1.T2.S3.

subtasks.title: Config unit tests + Mode A docs/configuration.md [generation] table
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: research-generate-config.md §2/§3 — existing config tests assert Defaults() values and file-override behavior (mirror the TokenLimit/MaxDuplicateRetries test cases). The bool-sentinel limitation (false-in-file ignored) is shared with AutoStageAll and is an accepted v1 limitation.

LOGIC: Add unit tests in internal/config: (a) Defaults() returns MultiTurnFallback==true && MultiTurnChunkTokens==32000; (b) a TOML file with [generation] multi_turn_chunk_tokens=16000 materializes to 16000 (int override honored); (c) a TOML file with multi_turn_fallback=true materializes to true (bool override honored); (d) a TOML file with multi_turn_fallback=false leaves the resolved value at the default true (documenting the accepted limitation — assert this explicitly so it is a known, tested behavior, not a silent bug); (e) overlay precedence: repo-file value overrides global-file value for chunk tokens.

Update the commented `[generation]` template block to include both keys. Add a one-line FR-T12 note: these do NOT interact with token_limit (multi-turn uses the UNTRUNCATED payload). Surface the multi_turn_fallback=false limitation honestly (cannot disable via file in this revision).

tasks.title: Generate core: chunking + N+1 turn protocol + trigger gate + failure→rescue + progress + verbose (R3)
tasks.description: The heart of the feature. Create a new internal/generate/multiturn.go with the chunk-sizing helper and the N+1 turn protocol, then wire the FR-T1 trigger gate into CommitStaged at the one-shot-exhaustion→rescue boundary (generate.go:288). The protocol reuses provider.Execute (one call per turn, inheriting per-turn verbose for free), provider.ParseOutput (final turn only), the dedupe path, and *RescueError (byte-identical on failure — FR-T7). Resolves the payload loop-scope issue (payload is loop-local at generate.go:228). Adds the FR-T5 progress line and FR-T11 verbose trigger line.

subtasks.title: multiturn.go chunk-sizing helper (EstimateTokens ceil, newline-anchored, PART i/N prefix)
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: research-generate-config.md §4 — git.EstimateTokens is at internal/git/tokens.go:25, signature `func EstimateTokens(s string) int`, formula ceil(runeCount/4) (RUNE-based, not byte — UTF-8/CJK does not over-count). This is the SINGLE token estimator; use it for both sizing and the gate (do NOT introduce a second estimator). Also EstimateTokensBytes([]byte) at tokens.go:32.
2. INPUT: PRD §9.24 FR-T3 (chunk sizing: N = ceil(payload_tokens/chunk_tokens); split into N consecutive chunks ≤ budget; anchor each boundary FORWARD to the next newline so no diff line fractures; each chunk carries a one-line "PART i/N:" prefix emitted OUTSIDE the budget). The payload string (the captured diff payload — passed in by the caller in P1.M1.T3.S2).
3. LOGIC: Create internal/generate/multiturn.go. Define a chunk type (e.g. `type chunk struct { index, total int; text string }`) and a pure helper `func chunkPayload(payload string, chunkTokens int) []chunk`. Compute N = ceilDiv(EstimateTokens(payload), chunkTokens) (N ≥ 1; if payload_tokens ≤ chunkTokens, N = 1 — a single chunk). Walk the payload in rune-count-sized windows of ≤ chunkTokens, advancing each boundary FORWARD to the next '\n' (so no line is fractured; the boundary line stays in the current chunk). Build each chunk's text as `fmt.Sprintf("PART %d/%d:\n%s", i, N, body)` where the PART prefix is OUTSIDE the body budget (body ≤ chunkTokens; the prefix adds its own tokens on top — acceptable, it is one short line). If N==1 the single chunk still carries the PART 1/1 prefix for protocol uniformity (the priming preamble references N).
4. OUTPUT: chunkPayload(...) []chunk — a pure, unit-testable helper. Consumed by P1.M1.T3.S2.
5. MOCKING: none — pure string math; depends only on git.EstimateTokens.
6. DOCS: none — how-it-works doc rides with P1.M1.T3.S4.

subtasks.title: multiturn.go N+1 turn protocol (Run): session id, priming, per-turn Execute, final parse
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: research-provider.md §5 — provider.Execute is `func Execute(ctx context.Context, spec CmdSpec, timeout time.Duration, vb *ui.Verbose) (stdout string, stderr string, err error)` at executor.go:44; it SHADOWS ctx with context.WithTimeout when timeout>0 and returns (stdout, stderr, err) EVEN ON ERROR; check errors.Is(err, context.DeadlineExceeded) for timeout. ParseOutput is `func ParseOutput(raw string, m Manifest) (msg string, ok bool, fellback bool)` at parse.go:41. research-generate-config.md §5 — *RescueError{Kind: ErrRescue,...} (generate.go:82–99) is the failure return; Unwrap returns Kind. fr-t9-verification.md — the verified turn protocol (sys prompt via flag on turn 1 only).
2. INPUT: P1.M1.T1.S3 (RenderMultiTurn), P1.M1.T2.S1 (cfg.MultiTurnChunkTokens), P1.M1.T3.S1 (chunkPayload). PRD §9.24 FR-T2 (lossless — same captured payload, no token_limit water-fill), FR-T4 (N+1 turn protocol verbatim), FR-T5 (per-turn timeout = cfg.Timeout), FR-T7 (failure → rescue), FR-T10 (message role only).
3. LOGIC: In internal/generate/multiturn.go add `func Run(ctx context.Context, deps generate.Deps, cfg config.Config, manifest provider.Manifest, sysPrompt, payload, msgModel, msgReasoning string) (msg string, ok bool, cause error)`. Steps: (1) chunks := chunkPayload(payload, cfg.MultiTurnChunkTokens); N := len(chunks). (2) sessionID := "stagehand-" + <fresh uuid> (use crypto/rand or the existing uuid helper if one exists in the repo; mint per-run, never resumed). (3) Priming preamble (verbatim with N interpolated): "I will send a git diff in N parts. After each part, reply with exactly: ok. Do not analyze or write any commit message until I explicitly ask at the end." (4) Turn 1: spec, rerr := manifest.RenderMultiTurn(msgModel, sysPrompt, preamble+"\n\n"+chunks[0].text, msgReasoning, sessionID, 1); on rerr return ("", false, rerr). (5) out, _, execErr := provider.Execute(ctx, *spec, cfg.Timeout, deps.Verbose); if errors.Is(execErr, context.DeadlineExceeded) || errors.Is(execErr, context.Canceled) || (execErr != nil && !is just a non-zero-exit-with-partial-stdout) → return ("", false, execErr) [per FR-T7 — any turn timeout/error aborts; treat non-zero exit as failure too per FR-T7 "a turn's provider error (non-zero exit that is not a timeout)"]. Discard out (the "ok"). (6) Turns 2..N: RenderMultiTurn(..., "", chunks[i-1].text, ..., sessionID, i) [sysPrompt="" → no system flag, turn>1]; Execute; discard "ok". (7) Turn N+1 (final): finalInstruction := "Now write the commit message for the diff above. Output ONLY the message."; spec := RenderMultiTurn(msgModel, "", finalInstruction, msgReasoning, sessionID, N+1); out, _, execErr := Execute(...). (8) On execErr return ("", false, execErr). (9) m, parseOK, _ := provider.ParseOutput(out, manifest); return (m, parseOK, nil) — the CALLER (P1.M1.T3.S3) runs dedupe on the returned msg via the existing path. NOTE: do NOT fork ParseOutput or dedupe — return the raw (msg, ok) and let CommitStaged's existing dedupe handle it.
4. OUTPUT: multiturn.Run returns (msg, ok, cause) for CommitStaged. cause is non-nil only on abort (turn error/timeout) — then CommitStaged maps it to &RescueError{Cause: cause}.
5. MOCKING: provider.Execute is the seam; the stub agent (cmd/stubagent) simulates append via per-turn scripted output ("ok"..."ok"...<message>). No real agent in CI.
6. DOCS: none — how-it-works doc rides with P1.M1.T3.S4.

subtasks.title: Wire FR-T1 trigger gate into CommitStaged + resolve payload scope + progress + verbose
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: research-generate-config.md §1 — the one-shot retry loop is generate.go:226–287; exhaustion falls to `if !success { return Result{}, &RescueError{Kind: ErrRescue, TreeSHA: treeSHA, ParentSHA: parentSHA, Candidate: candidate, Cause: lastCause} }` at generate.go:288–292. ⚠️ payload is LOOP-SCOPED (generate.go:228, `:=` inside the loop) so it is NOT in scope at :288; sysPrompt (:170), diff (:175), treeSHA, parentSHA, candidate, lastCause, msgModel, msgReasoning ARE in scope. research-tests-ui.md §3/§4 — provider.Execute already emits per-turn verbose (FR-T11 free); CommitStaged does NOT print progress itself (only the nil-safe Deps.Progress hook callback), so the FR-T5 progress line needs a direct stderr write or a new Deps callback.
2. INPUT: P1.M1.T1.S1 (SessionMode), P1.M1.T2.S1 (cfg.MultiTurnFallback/ChunkTokens), P1.M1.T3.S2 (multiturn.Run). PRD §9.24 FR-T1 (a–d trigger gate), FR-T2 (capture-once — same payload, no token_limit water-fill), FR-T5 (progress line), FR-T7 (failure → existing rescue byte-identical), FR-T11 (verbose), FR-T12 (token_limit non-interaction).
3. LOGIC: In internal/generate/generate.go CommitStaged: (a) RESOLVE PAYLOAD SCOPE — hoist `var payload string` before the loop (line ~225) and assign it inside the loop (`payload = prompt.BuildUserPayload(diff, cfg.Context, rejected)` then the retryInstr prepend), so the last-built payload survives to :288. This honors "do not recompute" (the multi-turn path reads the existing payload variable, NOT a fresh BuildUserPayload call). (b) INSERT TRIGGER GATE between the loop close (:287) and `if !success` (:288): replace the bare `if !success { return … &RescueError }` with `if !success { if cfg.MultiTurnFallback && git.EstimateTokens(payload) > cfg.MultiTurnChunkTokens && deps.Manifest.Resolve().SessionMode != nil && *deps.Manifest.Resolve().SessionMode == "append" { <run multi-turn; see below> }; return Result{}, &RescueError{Kind: ErrRescue, TreeSHA: treeSHA, ParentSHA: parentSHA, Candidate: candidate, Cause: lastCause} }`. Condition (a) "loop exhausted on empty/unparseable" is already true at !success. (c) MULTI-TURN RUN: emit the FR-T5 progress line to stderr (one line: fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "↳ falling back to multi-turn: %d turns, ~%s total\n", N+1, <duration estimate>) — N computed via multiturn.ChunkCount or EstimateTokens; total = cfg.Timeout × (N+1) formatted e.g. "12m"). Emit a verbose trigger line via deps.Verbose ("one-shot exhausted → multi-turn fallback"). Call msg2, ok2, cause := multiturn.Run(ctx, deps, cfg, deps.Manifest, sysPrompt, payload, msgModel, msgReasoning). If ok2 { run the EXISTING duplicate check on msg2 (IsDuplicate(ExtractSubject(msg2), recent)); if not duplicate → msg = FinalizeMessage(msg2, cfg); success = true; skip the rescue return. If duplicate → fall through to rescue (Candidate = msg2). } If !ok2 → set lastCause = cause (or cause if non-nil else lastCause); candidate = msg2 if msg2 != ""; fall through to the EXISTING &RescueError return (byte-identical — FR-T7). IMPORTANT: the multi-turn path does NOT re-check token_limit (FR-T12) — payload is the untruncated captured payload.
4. OUTPUT: CommitStaged gains the multi-turn branch; on success the normal CommitTree→UpdateRefCAS→Result path runs unchanged; on failure the existing rescue return fires byte-identically. No new exit codes, no rescue-message changes.
5. MOCKING: provider.Execute + the stub agent simulate the turns; CommitStaged's deps.Manifest is the stub manifest.
6. DOCS: none — how-it-works doc rides with P1.M1.T3.S4.

subtasks.title: Unit tests (chunk math, trigger truth table, token_limit non-interaction) + Mode A how-it-works.md
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: research-tests-ui.md §1/§2 — TestCommitStaged_DedupeRetryThenSuccess (generate_test.go:118) uses stubtest.NewScript for per-turn output; the idempotent-index invariant (TestCommitStaged_IdempotentIndexOnFailure :318) snapshots git diff --cached --name-only before/after. research-generate-config.md §1c — EstimateTokens is not yet called directly on the payload in generate.go (only via the reserve seam) — these are the first direct payload-token calls.
2. INPUT: P1.M1.T3.S1 (chunkPayload), P1.M1.T3.S3 (trigger gate). PRD §9.24 FR-T1 (a–d), FR-T3, FR-T12.
3. LOGIC: Add internal/generate/multiturn_test.go: (a) chunk-count ceil math — payload of exactly chunkTokens → N=1; payload of chunkTokens+1 → N=2; payload of 2.5×chunkTokens → N=3 (ceil). (b) newline-anchored boundaries — construct a payload where the naive byte/rune boundary would land mid-line and assert every chunk boundary in the output falls on a '\n' (no fractured diff line). (c) PART i/N prefix outside budget — assert each chunk.text starts with "PART i/N:\n" and i/total are correct and monotonic. (d) trigger-gate truth table — a table test exercising each of the four FR-T1 conditions independently flipping to false → multi-turn skipped → existing rescue (mock multiturn.Run or assert it was NOT called): (i) MultiTurnFallback=false; (ii) EstimateTokens(payload) ≤ MultiTurnChunkTokens (small payload); (iii) SessionMode="" (non-append provider); (iv) success already true (loop succeeded — gate not reached). (e) token_limit non-interaction (FR-T12) — set cfg.TokenLimit to a small value AND a payload exceeding it; assert multi-turn still uses the UNTRUNCATED payload (the chunk count reflects the full payload, not the token_limit-truncated one). These are unit tests on the pure helpers + the gate predicate; the full end-to-end integration is P1.M1.T4.
4. OUTPUT: passing unit tests; green `go test ./internal/generate/...`.
5. DOCS: Mode A (rides with this work) — docs/how-it-works.md: add a concise "Multi-turn fallback" subsection under the generation section. Cover: when it triggers (the four FR-T1 conditions — one-shot exhausted, payload > one chunk, multi_turn_fallback on, provider session_mode="append"); that chunking is lossless (the full diff is re-delivered across N+1 session turns, NOT summarized — contrast the rejected lossy map-reduce); the N+1 turn protocol (priming preamble, PART i/N chunks, final "write the message" turn); that the final turn reuses the normal parse + duplicate-rejection pipeline; that failure falls back to the standard rescue (snapshot safe). Note token_limit does not apply to multi-turn (FR-T12). Cross-link PRD §9.24.

tasks.title: Integration tests: stub multi-turn end-to-end (R4)
tasks.description: Extend the existing internal/stubtest stub-agent pattern to drive the multi-turn path end-to-end against a temp git repo. The stub already varies output per-invocation (NewScript, call-order indexed) and captures the rendered argv (STAGEHAND_STUB_ARGSFILE) — sufficient to assert --session-id presence and --no-session absence without true session recall (the orchestrator re-sends content). Covers the FR-T4 happy path (N+1 turns → one commit) and the FR-T7 failure paths (mid-turn exit → rescue; small-payload skip; non-append provider skip), plus the idempotent-index invariant.

subtasks.title: Integration: N+1 turns, --session-id present, --no-session dropped, final parsed+deduped, commit lands
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: research-tests-ui.md §1/§2 — internal/stubtest/stubtest.go: NewScript(t, bin, []string{...}) returns successive outputs (call-order indexed via a file-backed counter STAGEHAND_STUB_COUNTER, clamps to last); STAGEHAND_STUB_ARGSFILE writes the rendered argv NUL-joined so a test can read it and split on "\x00". cmd/stubagent/main.go is env-driven (drains stdin, writes marker/argsfile, sleeps, emits stderr, then stdout = OUT or script-indexed line, then os.Exit). TestCommitStaged_DedupeRetryThenSuccess (generate_test.go:118) is the canonical per-turn-output template; TestCommitStaged_MessageRoleOverride (:339) shows the ArgsFile argv-capture pattern (read args, sliceContains). The stub need NOT recall content — the orchestrator re-sends prior chunks; the stub just needs to return "ok" for priming turns and the message on the final turn.
2. INPUT: P1.M1.T3.S3 (the wired CommitStaged), P1.M1.T1.S4 (pi SessionMode="append"). PRD §9.24 FR-T4/T6.
3. LOGIC: Add an integration test in internal/generate (e.g. generate_multiturn_test.go). Build the stub (stubtest.Build(t)), init a temp repo, seed a commit, write+stage a LARGE file (enough that EstimateTokens(payload) > MultiTurnChunkTokens — e.g. set cfg.MultiTurnChunkTokens to a tiny value like 50 so even a modest diff exceeds one chunk, forcing N≥2). Configure a stub manifest with SessionMode="append" (use stubtest.Manifest then set the field, OR a helper) and a NewScript of ["ok", "ok", ..., "feat: add big thing"] (N "ok" lines for turns 1..N, then the message on turn N+1 — clamp covers extra calls). Drive one-shot to exhaustion first: set the stub's one-shot output to empty/invalid so the loop exhausts (e.g. the FIRST script line is "" → parse fails → loop exhausts → multi-turn triggers). Assert: (a) the commit lands (res.CommitSHA matches shaRe, headSHA == res.CommitSHA, git log round-trips the message); (b) the stub was invoked N+1 times (read the counter file or count ArgsFile writes); (c) via ArgsFile, EVERY turn's argv contains "--session-id", <a stable value> and does NOT contain "--no-session"; (d) turn 1's argv contains the system-prompt flag, later turns do not. NOTE: because MultiTurnChunkTokens is tiny, the priming may produce many turns — keep the script list long enough or rely on the clamp-to-last for the message.
4. OUTPUT: a green integration test proving the multi-turn happy path produces a single correct commit with the verified render contract.
5. MOCKING: the stub agent (cmd/stubagent) is the only "agent"; no real provider in CI. The temp git repo is real (matches the existing generate_test.go harness).
6. DOCS: none — test-only.

subtasks.title: Integration: mid-turn failure → rescue; small-payload skip; non-append provider skip
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: research-tests-ui.md §2 — TestCommitStaged_ParseFailRescue (generate_test.go:162) and TestCommitStaged_IdempotentIndexOnFailure (:318) are the rescue + idempotent-index templates (snapshot git diff --cached --name-only before/after; assert HEAD + staged diff unchanged after a *RescueError). research-generate-config.md §5 — multi-turn failure returns &RescueError{Kind: ErrRescue,...} byte-identical to one-shot; use ErrRescue (exit 3), NOT ErrTimeout.
2. INPUT: P1.M1.T4.S1 (the happy-path harness). PRD §9.24 FR-T1 (small-payload skip), FR-T7 (failure→rescue), FR-T8 (non-append skip).
3. LOGIC: Add integration tests in internal/generate: (a) MID-TURN FAILURE → RESCUE: stub SessionMode="append", large payload (N≥2), script = ["ok", <exit 1 on turn 2 via STAGEHAND_STUB_EXIT or a non-zero exit>]. Assert CommitStaged returns *RescueError{Kind: ErrRescue} with TreeSHA == the frozen snapshot; assert the idempotent-index invariant (headSHA and `git diff --cached --name-only` unchanged before/after). (b) SMALL-PAYLOAD SKIP: stub SessionMode="append", but a TINY diff (EstimateTokens(payload) ≤ MultiTurnChunkTokens) and one-shot output empty → the loop exhausts, multi-turn is SKIPPED (condition b false), and the existing rescue fires (assert *RescueError; assert the multi-turn stub turns were NOT invoked — counter stays 0 or no ArgsFile beyond the one-shot). (c) NON-APPEND PROVIDER SKIP: stub SessionMode="" (e.g. a claude-shaped stub), large payload, one-shot empty → multi-turn skipped silently (condition d false), existing rescue fires. For all three, assert the rescue message/exit behavior matches the one-shot-exhaustion path (no new exit codes, no message changes).
4. OUTPUT: green integration tests proving FR-T7 (failure is pure upside — never worse than one-shot-exhausted) and the FR-T1 gate's negative conditions.
5. MOCKING: stub agent + temp git repo (existing harness).
6. DOCS: none — test-only.

tasks.title: Sync changeset-level documentation (Mode B)

The per-file Mode A docs (providers.md, configuration.md provider + [generation] sections, how-it-works.md multi-turn subsection) ride with their implementing subtasks (T1.S5, T2.S3, T3.S4). This final task handles the cross-cutting/overview docs that only make sense once the whole feature is in place: the README.md feature blurb (user-visible reliability win for large diffs) and a consistency check of FUTURE_SPEC.md (already updated — confirm, do not edit unless inconsistent).

subtasks.title: README.md — add multi-turn fallback feature blurb
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: README.md is the marketing surface (PRD §21.5). The multi-turn fallback is a user-visible reliability win for large diffs and belongs in the feature surface / "how it works" area. Keep it to one sentence — do not expand scope.
2. INPUT: the complete, tested feature (P1.M1.T1–T4). PRD §9.24, §5 (UVP), §21.5 (README structure).
3. LOGIC: In README.md, add a one-line feature blurb for the multi-turn fallback in the feature-list / "how it works" area. Suggested wording (adapt to the README's existing voice): "Lossless multi-turn fallback: when a one-shot generation of a large diff fails, stagehand re-delivers the full diff across session turns so a single message still lands (no truncation, no extra commits)." Do NOT add CLI flags or config details beyond a pointer to docs (the feature is internally triggered; the only user surfaces are the progress line and the two config keys documented in docs/configuration.md).
4. OUTPUT: README.md updated with a concise feature mention.
5. DOCS: this IS the doc task (Mode B).
